info.title: Kluvs API
info.description: Supabase Edge Functions API for managing book clubs.

get.description: Get a club by ID, or find a club by Discord channel ID + Server ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the club

parameters.name: discord_channel
parameters.description: Query by Discord Channel ID (requires server_id)

parameters.name: server_id
parameters.description: The Discord Server ID (required if querying by channel)

post.description: Creates a club, optionally with members, an active session (book + discussions), and a shame list.

active_session.description: Immediately start a session for this club.

shame_list.description: List of Member IDs to immediately add to the shame list.

put.description: Update club details like name, channel, or shame list.

join_policy.description: Controls how members can join the club. Setting to INVITE_LINK creates (or reuses) an active club invite token; setting to PRIVATE deactivates all active invites for the club.

club_updated.description: Whether the clubs table row was updated.

shame_list_updated.description: Whether the shame list membership changed.

delete.description: Permanently remove a club and all its history.

schema.description: Optional security check to ensure club belongs to server.

get.description: Get member details by internal integer ID, linked Auth User ID (UUID), or Discord ID. Alternatively, use the `search` parameter to find members by name or handle — useful for invite flows. The `search` and lookup parameters (`id`, `user_id`, `discord_id`) are mutually exclusive; `search` takes priority when present.

schema.description: The linked Supabase Auth User ID.
Case-insensitive substring search across member name and handle. Returns up to 10 results. Use with `exclude_club_id` to filter out existing members.
Only valid alongside `search`. When provided, members already belonging to this club are excluded from search results.

clubs.description: Clubs this member belongs to, with their per-club role.

role.description: Member's role in this specific club

shame_clubs.description: List of clubs where this member is currently on the shame list.

post.description: Creates a new member and links them to specific clubs.

clubs.description: Array of Club IDs this member belongs to. Optional, but if present must contain at least one club ID (an empty array is rejected).

avatar_path.description: Path to the avatar image in storage.

discord_id.description: Discord user snowflake ID. Stored as text to preserve precision.

platform_metadata.description: Flexible JSON object for platform-specific data (e.g., Discord Top.gg votes). Defaults to {}.

id.description: Optional explicit ID. Omit to let the DB sequence assign one.

400.description: Missing name or invalid clubs array

404.description: One or more provided Club IDs do not exist

put.description: Update member details. Can update basic fields, add/remove club associations, update per-club roles, or add a member to a specific club via the `add_to_club` action. The `add_to_club` action is mutually exclusive with the other update paths — when present, only the club-add logic runs. It requires the caller to be an admin or owner of the target club.

id.description: The ID of the member to update (or the target member for add_to_club).

avatar_path.description: Storage path of the member's avatar. Must start with `{id}/` (e.g. `42/avatar.png`). Pass null to clear the avatar.

discord_id.description: Discord user snowflake ID. Pass null to clear it.

platform_metadata.description: Flexible JSON object for platform-specific data. Replaces the entire field — callers must Read-Modify-Write.

clubs.description: Full list of Club IDs this member should belong to. Diffed against existing to add/remove.

club_roles.description: Map of club_id → role to update per-club roles. Valid roles are admin and member (owner cannot be assigned or changed via this endpoint).

add_to_club.description: Club ID to add the target member (`id`) to. When present, all other update fields are ignored. The calling user's JWT must belong to a member who is an admin or owner of the target club. The target member is added with the `member` role.

200.description: Member updated, or member added to club successfully.

400.description: Invalid input — e.g. id is not a positive integer, clubs is not an array, one or more club IDs in `clubs` do not exist, club_roles is malformed or references an invalid/owner role, the member is not part of a club referenced in club_roles, or add_to_club is empty.

401.description: Missing or invalid Authorization header (add_to_club only)

403.description: Caller is not an admin or owner of the target club (add_to_club only)

404.description: Member not found (target member for the update, or for add_to_club), or the club referenced by add_to_club does not exist.

409.description: Target member is already in the club (add_to_club only)

delete.description: Delete a member and all their data. For each club the member owns: if they are the sole member the club is deleted; otherwise ownership is transferred to an admin, or to a random member if no admin exists. The linked auth account is also deleted if one exists. This is the canonical deletion path — do not delete auth accounts directly.

get.description: Fetches all servers (with their clubs) OR a specific server if 'id' is provided.

schema.description: Optional Server ID. If omitted, returns all servers.

200.description: Server list or specific server details

oneOf.description: Response when ID is provided (Single Server)

member_count.description: Number of members in this club

latest_session.description: The club's most recent session by due_date, with nested book title/author, or null if none exist

books.description: Joined book info for the session

oneOf.description: Response when ID is omitted (List of Servers)

post.description: Registers a new Discord server in the database.

id.description: Optional Discord Snowflake ID. If omitted, a random ID is generated.

put.description: Update the name of an existing server.

400.description: Missing ID or no fields to update

delete.description: Deletes a server. Fails if the server still contains clubs.

400.description: Cannot delete server because it has existing clubs

schema.description: Custom error shape (not the standard Error schema)

error.example: Cannot delete server with existing clubs. Please delete all clubs first.

clubs_count.description: Number of clubs still associated with this server

get.description: When `reading_log=true` is present, returns all sessions the authenticated member is actively reading (`is_reading=true`), grouped by status (`active` / `finished`), across all their clubs. Requires a valid user JWT — bot callers receive 403.

200.description: Reading log grouped by session status

403.description: Bot callers not permitted

404.description: No member record found for the authenticated user

post.description: Create a new reading session for a club. Prefer `book_id` (a registered book's local DB ID). The inline `book` object is supported as a deprecated fallback.

club_id.description: The ID of the club hosting this session.

book_id.description: Preferred. The local DB ID of a pre-registered book (from POST /book).

due_date.description: Target date for finishing the book.

book.description: Deprecated. Inline book object. Use book_id instead.

all_reading.description: Controls the default `is_reading` flag for auto-populated session_members rows (one per existing club member). When true (default), all members are marked as reading and their shelf is set to "currently_reading" for this book. Set to false to default new session members to not-reading.

discussions.description: Optional list of discussions to schedule immediately.

scheduled_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (with timezone offset) for the discussion

400.description: Missing required fields (club_id, or neither book_id nor book)

404.description: Club not found, or book_id not found

put.description: Update session details, including nested book updates.

book.description: Fields to update on the associated book.

session_members.description: Upsert participation flags for session members. Reading members have their book moved to the "currently_reading" shelf; opted-out members have their session-derived "currently_reading" shelf entry removed. If this is the only field provided (alongside `id`), any club member may make this request; otherwise owner/admin is required.

finish.description: If true, marks the session as finished (active -> finished, idempotent guard returns 400 if already finished), increments books_read for all session_members with is_reading=true, and moves their book to the "read" shelf. When provided as true, this is handled as a standalone action and short-circuits other updates in the same request.

discussions.description: Reserved for future use. Currently only checked for presence to determine the required role (owner/admin) for this request; no discussion records are created, updated, or deleted by this endpoint.

discussion_ids_to_delete.description: Reserved for future use. Currently only checked for presence to determine the required role (owner/admin) for this request; no discussion records are deleted by this endpoint.

members_credited.description: Number of is_reading session_members whose books_read count was incremented

oneOf.description: Response when no changes were applicable

book.description: Whether the associated book was updated

session.description: Whether session fields (club_id, due_date) were updated

400.description: Session is already finished (when `finish` is true and session.status is not 'active')

404.description: Session not found, or target club not found (when changing club_id)

delete.description: Delete a session and its discussions. The associated book is kept in the library.

get.description:
Three modes:
- `?q=...` — Search Google Books API by query string
- `?isbn=...` — Look up a book by ISBN via Google Books API
- `?id=...` — Get a locally registered book by its DB ID

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max results per page for search (default 10, max 40)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-based index of the first result to return (for pagination)

200.description:
Book(s) found. For ?q= search results, `total` is Google Books' estimated corpus
count (not the number of items returned on this page — use `books.length` for that).
The estimate is approximate and Google caps actual delivery at ~1,000 items regardless
of the reported total.

500.description: Internal error or missing API key

post.description:
Register a book in the local database (typically after the client selects it from
a Google Books search). If `external_google_id` is provided and already exists,
returns the existing record with `created: false` (idempotent).

external_google_id.description: Google Books volume ID (recommended for deduplication)

post.summary: Join a club via invite link
post.description: Authenticates the caller using the Bearer JWT, validates the invite token, and adds the user to the club as a member. Increments use_count on the invite (best-effort — failure does not roll back the join).

400.description: Missing token or caller is already a member

403.description: Bot callers not permitted — join requires a user JWT

404.description: Invalid or expired invite, or member record not found

get.description: Validates an invite token and returns basic club info. No authentication required. Does not join the club or increment use_count.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: The invite token from the club's invite URL

get.summary: Get like status for a book
get.description: Returns whether the authenticated user has liked a given book.

403.description: Bot callers not permitted

404.description: Book not found, or no member record for the authenticated user

500.description: Database error while resolving the member record

post.description: Toggles a book like on or off for the authenticated user. Acts as a personal bookmark.

liked.description: New state after toggle (true = just liked, false = just unliked)

403.description: Bot callers not permitted

404.description: Book not found, or no member record for the authenticated user

post.description: Creates a discussion event for a session. The server generates the discussion ID.

403.description: Caller is not owner/admin of the session's club

put.description: Partially update a discussion's title, scheduled_at, or location.

403.description: Caller is not owner/admin of the session's club
Caller is not owner/admin of the session's club

post.summary: Set or change the caller's RSVP for a discussion
post.description: Upserts the calling member's RSVP status for a discussion. Each member has at most one RSVP row per discussion — calling this again overwrites the prior status. RSVPing "yes" also opts the member into the discussion's reading session (session_members.is_reading=true) and places that session's book on the member's currently_reading shelf, mirroring PUT /session's opt-in behavior. "no"/"maybe" have no effect on session participation. This side effect is best-effort.

403.description: Bot callers not permitted, or caller is not a member of the discussion's club

404.description: Discussion not found, or no member record for the authenticated user

get.summary: Get the RSVP roster for a discussion
get.description: Returns every club member's RSVP response for the given discussion (members with no row are treated as "hasn't responded"), the calling member's own status, and the club's total member count so the client can derive how many members haven't responded yet.

200.description: The roster, the caller's own status, and the club's total member count

403.description: Bot callers not permitted, or caller is not a member of the discussion's club

404.description: Discussion not found, or no member record for the authenticated user

delete.summary: Clear the caller's RSVP for a discussion
delete.description: Removes the calling member's own RSVP row for the given discussion, reverting them to "unanswered". A no-op (still 204) if no row exists.

403.description: Bot callers not permitted

404.description: No member record for the authenticated user

get.summary: Get the caller's note for a discussion
get.description: Returns the authenticated member's own private note for the given discussion, or null if they have not written one. Never returns other members' notes.

403.description: Bot callers not permitted

404.description: No member record for the authenticated user

post.description: Creates a private "food for thought" note for the calling member, tied to a discussion. Each member may have at most one note per discussion — use PUT to overwrite an existing note.

403.description: Bot callers not permitted

404.description: Discussion not found, or no member record for the authenticated user

409.description: A note already exists for this member and discussion — use PUT to overwrite

put.description: Overwrites the content of the calling member's own note and bumps updated_at. There is no append/multi-note model — this replaces the note's content.

403.description: Note exists but is not owned by the caller

404.description: Note not found, or no member record for the authenticated user

delete.description: Permanently removes the calling member's own note.

403.description: Note exists but is not owned by the caller

404.description: Note not found, or no member record for the authenticated user

get.summary: Get shelf assignments for the authenticated user
get.description:
Two modes:
- No `book_id` — returns all shelf assignments for the user, each joined with the full book record
- `?book_id=...` — returns the shelf assignment for that specific book, or `null` if not shelved

parameters.name: book_id
parameters.description: Local DB book ID. If provided, returns the shelf for that single book.

403.description: Bot callers not permitted

404.description: No member record found for the authenticated user

post.description: Assigns or updates the authenticated user's shelf for a book. One row per (member, book) pair — assigning a new shelf replaces the previous one.

403.description: Bot callers not permitted

get.summary: List reading progress entries for the authenticated user
get.description:
Returns the authenticated member's reading_progress rows, optionally filtered
by book, session, or status. Each entry is joined with its book's details.

parameters.name: book_id
parameters.description: Filter to entries for a specific book

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Filter to entries linked to a specific session

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter to entries with a specific status

400.description: book_id must be an integer, or status is invalid

403.description: Bot callers not permitted

404.description: No member record found for the authenticated user

post.summary: Log a new reading progress entry
post.description:
Creates a new in-progress reading attempt for the authenticated member.
Each call creates a new row — re-reads of the same book are separate entries.

session_id.description: Optional session this progress entry is linked to

400.description: Missing or invalid fields, or progress_type/value mismatch

403.description: Bot callers not permitted

put.description:
Updates progress for an existing entry owned by the authenticated member.
Sets `updated_at` on every update. If `status` is set to `completed`,
also sets `completed_at` to the current time.

`progress_type` is required on every update. An entry can switch
between page- and percent-based tracking by passing a different
`progress_type` than the entry currently has — the field for the
previous type is cleared (set to `null`). When switching, the entry
must already have (or this request must provide) a value for the new
type's field.

When `progress_type` is `page`, `percent_complete` is computed
automatically from `current_page` and the book's `page_count` (and
cannot be set directly). When `progress_type` is `percent`,
`current_page` cannot be set.

current_page.description: Required when progress_type is page, unless the entry already has one

percent_complete.description: Required when progress_type is percent, unless the entry already has one

403.description: Bot callers not permitted, or entry not owned by caller

delete.description: Removes a reading_progress entry owned by the authenticated member.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the reading_progress entry to delete

403.description: Bot callers not permitted, or entry not owned by caller

get.description: Returns all Discord guilds where the bot is present and the given member holds the MANAGE_GUILD permission, along with each guild's text channels. Used by the frontend to populate the server/channel selector when creating a club.

200.description: List of qualifying guilds with text channels, sorted by guild name.

403.description: Bot callers not permitted, or the authenticated user does not own the requested member_id.

500.description: Missing DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN env var, or Discord API error.

join_policy.description: Controls how new members can join. PRIVATE = by admin only; INVITE_LINK = anyone with the link.

invite_token.description: Active invite token UUID (present when join_policy is INVITE_LINK, null otherwise).

members.description: Club members with their per-club role.

shame_list.description: Array of member IDs on shame list

discord_id.description: Discord user snowflake ID. Stored as TEXT to preserve JSON precision.

platform_metadata.description: Flexible JSON object for platform-specific data (e.g., Discord Top.gg votes).

clubs.description: Clubs this member belongs to, each with their per-club role.

external_google_id.description: Google Books volume ID for deduplication

scheduled_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (with timezone offset) for the discussion

token.description: Shareable invite token used in invite URLs

created_by.description: Member ID who created the invite

is_reading.description: Whether the member is actively reading (vs. opted out)

ClubMember.description: A member as embedded within a club response, including their per-club role.

clubs.description: IDs of all clubs this member belongs to

ClubSummary.description: A lightweight club reference as embedded within a member response, including the member's role.

PastSession.description: A finished session as embedded within a club response.

BookSummary.description: A lightweight book reference as embedded within a reading progress entry.

source.description: How the shelf was set — 'manual' (via /shelf) or 'session' (auto-synced from session/discussion participation)
